comment: FR-AUD-002 / ADR-0015 uncorrelated crossfade: two different constant sources (A=0.8, B=0.6) joined by an equalPower crossfade of D=1/2 at t=1/2. Per ADR-0015 section 4 the region applies g_out(x)=cos(pi*x/2) to A's tail and g_in(x)=sin(pi*x/2) to B, with g_out^2+g_in^2=1 at every frame - constant power for uncorrelated program. Region x per frame = 0, 1/4, 1/2, 3/4 giving (g_out, g_in) = (1,0), (0.92387953,0.38268343), (0.70710678,0.70710678), (0.38268343,0.92387953) and reference 0.8*g_out+0.6*g_in = 0.8, 0.96871369, 0.98994949, 0.86047447. Discrimination: a linear rendering of the same pair yields 0.8, 0.75, 0.7, 0.65 (fails by 0.29 at midpoint); a sequential-fades notch rendering yields 0.6*g_in alone after the cut (0 at the boundary frame, fails by 0.8). Audio golden math is pure CPU rational/float arithmetic, machine-independent, so the tolerance stays at the suite-wide 1e-5.
